rulings.Case Title: DANIEL SANDOVAL VS. NORTH AMERICA VAN LINES, INC. A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Defendant North America Van Lines, Inc.S First Amended Answer To Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 13, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF DANIEL SANDOVAL'S Motion To Strike Defendant North America Van Lines, Inc.'s First Amended Answer To Plaintiff's Complaint. Denied. Although Defendant should have obtained leave of court before filing its amended answer, it would serve no practical purpose to strike the answer. Defendant would subsequently file a motion for leave to amend, which would almost certainly be granted, given there is no trial date set and discovery is still ongoing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: ATLAS TOWING SERVICES, INC VS. MCKINNEY VEHICLE SERVICES INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 13, 2015, Line 5, CROSS-DEFENDANT DONGBU INSURANCE COMPANY LTD'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend in part and overruled in part. The demurrer to the cause of action for Equitable Indemnity, Apportionment of Fault, and Contribution is sustained without leave to amend. The allegations in the First Amended Cross-Compliant ("FACC") do not establish that Cross-Complainant and Cross-Defendant are joint tortfeasors or co-obligors. Further, while the two insurance company Cross-Defendants could potentially state a cause of action for apportionment of fault or contribution against the other, none of the cases cited by Cross-Complainant support its argument that the insured can maintain this cause of action against its insurance companies. The demurrer is overruled as to the declaratory relief cause of action. Liberally construing the FACC, as the Court must do on demurrer, the FACC adequately alleges that there is an actual controversy between the parties about whether there is a duty to indemnify or defend. The demurrer is overruled as to the breach of contract cause of action. The FACC adequately pleads the legal effect of the contract and puts Cross-Defendant on notice of the claims against it. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR BRONEC VS. UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA AT SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday July 13, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, MONYCA DUARTE-ROBERTS Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SAM GLASMAN VS. YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday July 13, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT NINA SOCHILINA, YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE, INC. DBA YELLOW CAB MOTION TO COMPEL MENTAL EXAMINATION. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is DENIED; the Court will sign Plaintiff's Proposed Order & Protective Order. The Defendant has not demonstrated any compelling need for the broad inquiry beyond the injuries put at issue by the Plaintiff in this case. The scope of the inquiry proposed by the Defendant (Exhibit G) goes beyond what is directly relevant and is not narrowly tailored to the injuries at issue in the case. The Protective Order shields the dissemination of the raw data produced in Dr. Berg's examination. The Defendant has cited no authority to support its assertion that producing these data directly to Plaintiff's counsel would violate any ethical obligations. Further, any such obligations, to the extent they exist, are obviated by a Court order to produce the data. In reaching this decision, the Court did not consider Plaintiff's Exhibits 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 or 10 nor did the Court consider any of the Plaintiff's arguments based on those exhibits. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARY O'SHEA ET AL VS. REAL BRIGHT MEDIA INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Defendants Subpoena To Yahoo And Att Internet Services, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday July 13, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MARGIE BARR, DANIEL BARRETT, JASON BISHOP Motion To Quash Defendants Subpoena To Yahoo And Att Internet Services, And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. The Court intends to grant the Motion to Quash as to former and non-parties; the subpoenas in regard to these individuals are frivolous and an abuse of the discovery process. The Court further intends to deny to Plaintiff's request for damages under the Stored Communications Act (18 USC section 2702 et seq). Further argument and discussion is required in regard to the Motion to Quash insofar as it relates to the actual plaintiffs. Further argument is required in regard to the request for discovery sanctions under CCP section 2023.010 et seq. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ACADEMIC INNOVATIONS LLC VS. YOUNG SOON HWANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 13, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT YOUNG HWANG'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Denied. "When a defendant challenges the court's personal jurisdiction on the ground of improper service of process the burden is on the plaintiff to prove...the facts requisite to an effective service." (Summers v. McClanahan (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 403, 413.) Plaintiff has established that substitute service was properly effectuated. The evidence submitted shows that Defendant and Sung Hwan Hwang owned the property in Saratoga where the papers were served. (Supplemental Declaration of Tony Klein, pp. 3-4.) The individual who was given the papers identified himself as Sung Hwan Hwang, Defendant's husband and therefore a competent member of the household. (Id., p. 4-5.) This is sufficient to meet Plaintiff's burden, particularly in light of the fact that Defendant has submitted no admissible evidence about where Defendant resides or whether Sung Hwan Hwang is her husband. The Declarations of Michael Hwang are not executed under penalty of perjury and can therefore not be considered. (See CCP sec. 2015.5.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CALEB DUBOIS ET AL VS. COMCAST CORPORATION, A PENNSYLVANIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of The First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 13, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT COMCAST CABLE COMMUNICATIONS MANAGEMENT, LLC'S Motion To Strike Portions Of The First Amended Complaint. The motion is granted with 20 days leave to amend as to the tolling allegations. Plaintiff has leave to allege that tolling exists through the date of the decertification order, December 15, 2015. See Hall v. Variable Annuity Life Ins. Co., 727 F.3d 372, 375 (5th Cir. 2013); Giovanniello v. ALM Media, LLC, 726 F.3d 106, 117 (2nd Cir. 2013). The remainder of the motion is denied. Defendant has improperly raised the collateral estoppel issue in the Reply, not in the initial motion. Matter on calendar for Monday, July 13, 2015, Line 8, DEFENDANT BRIAN GLICK'S Motion To Quash. The motion to quash service is denied. The contract indicates that defendant entered into the agreement in his individual capacity and defendant consented to California jurisdiction based on the forum selection clause (par. 10 of the Attorney Membership Agreement). Defendant was properly served at his work address. The mail service was proper per CCP 415.40 because defendant was out of the state.
